content.problemStatement:
Chef is an expert baker. Each cake costs him 303030 coins to make, and then he sells each for 505050 coins.

Chef is going to sell cakes for NNN days, numbered 1,2,…,N1, 2, \ldots, N1,2,…,N. On the iii-th day, AiA_iAi​ customers will visit Chef's shop and buy exactly 111 cake each (if Chef has any cakes available, that is).

However, Chef preferring uniformity, will always produce the same number of cakes at the start of every day, say XXX. Because cakes become stale fast, he cannot keep the current day's cake for the next day. You want to decide this non-negative integer XXX in such a way that it maximizes Chef's profit over all NNN days.


Input Format

The first line of input will contain a single integer TTT, denoting the number of test cases.
Each test case consists of multiple lines of input.

The first line of each test case contains a single integer NNN - the number of days.
The second line contains NNN integers - A1,A2,…,ANA_1, A_2, \ldots, A_NA1​,A2​,…,AN​.




Output Format
For each test case, output on a new line the maximum profit possible if we suitably decide the integer XXX.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Test Case 1 : Chef chooses X=1X = 1X=1, i.e. he makes 111 cake every day. On each day, exactly 111 customer comes to Chef's shop, and he is able to sell the 111 cake he made. His total costs is 30⋅3=9030 \cdot 3 = 9030⋅3=90 coins, while he gets 50⋅3=15050 \cdot 3 = 15050⋅3=150 coins from selling his cakes. Thus, he makes a profit of 606060 coins.
Test Case 2 : If Chef chose X=1X = 1X=1, he would get 606060 coins profit. Instead, if Chef chose X=2X = 2X=2, he is able to sell 2+2+1=52 + 2 + 1 = 52+2+1=5 cakes for 250250250 coins, while they cost him 180180180 coins. Thus, here Chef has a profit of 707070 coins which is the maximum possible.
